name: Vehicle Braking
description: A vehicle reduces velocity, reverses, or locks traction according to brake input, surface grip, and wheel state.
design_notes: Braking needs clear separation between service brake, reverse throttle, handbrake, and collision stopping. The design should define brake_input, brake_bias, tire grip, surface friction, anti-lock behavior, and how braking interacts with drifting, boost cancellation, and network prediction.
design_purpose: Give players control over stopping distance, corner entry, reverse movement, and vehicle safety.
Create handling skill through braking points, traction management, and handbrake turns.
Support vehicle damage, fuel, AI driving, and accessibility assists with consistent deceleration rules.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can slow, stop, and set up turns with deliberate control.

parameters.name: vehicle_id
parameters.description: Vehicle identifier used by brake state, damage, saves, and authority checks.

parameters.name: brake_input
parameters.description: Normalized primary braking input captured for the current simulation tick.

parameters.name: handbrake_input
parameters.description: Secondary brake control used for rear lock, drift initiation, or parking behavior.

parameters.name: brake_bias_front_ratio
parameters.description: Distribution of braking authority toward front wheels or forward contact points.

parameters.name: abs_assist_profile_id
parameters.description: Anti-lock or assist profile that limits wheel lock under heavy braking.

parameters.name: reverse_engage_threshold_kph
parameters.description: Forward velocity below which brake input may become reverse throttle.

edge_cases: Brake input is held while the vehicle is airborne or has only one wheel touching ground.
The player switches from reverse to forward throttle while sliding downhill.
Handbrake input overlaps with drift scoring, boost cancellation, or damage-induced wheel loss.
A network correction arrives while the client predicted a handbrake turn.
common_bugs: Braking applies a direct velocity clamp and ignores traction, causing instant stops on ice.
Reverse engages while the vehicle is still moving forward too quickly.
Handbrake state remains active after seat exit or input device disconnect.
Brake lights, audio, and physics use different thresholds and disagree visually.
balancing_notes: Short stopping distances improve accessibility but reduce racing line skill.
Handbrakes should be useful for tight turns without becoming faster than normal cornering everywhere.
Reverse behavior should be predictable in combat and traversal spaces where backing out matters.
accessibility_notes: Offer braking assist or auto-brake options for players who struggle with analog timing.
Keep camera shake and skid audio adjustable during heavy braking.

implementation_notes.unity: Apply brake_input in FixedUpdate through WheelCollider brake torque or a custom contact model, using surface grip and brake_bias_front_ratio. Store handbrake and reverse state in rollback snapshots with driver authority.
implementation_notes.godot: VehicleBody3D or custom controllers should compute braking from contact state and ground surface, not render frames. Keep reverse engagement as an explicit state so input UI, lights, and physics remain synchronized.
implementation_notes.unreal: Chaos Vehicles expose brake and handbrake inputs, but gameplay code should validate driver_player_id and assist profiles before applying them. Replicate compressed input and reconcile pose, wheel slip, and brake state from server corrections.
implementation_notes.web: Run braking in the fixed simulation pass and clamp by traction rather than raw velocity. For online play, keep brake_input and handbrake_input in input frames so prediction can be replayed after correction.

relationships.reason: Braking defines how acceleration is reduced, reversed, or converted into controlled stopping distance.
Brake and counter-steer input often initiate or control drift state.
Rapid braking and FOV changes need smoothing rules to avoid uncomfortable camera pulses.

example_games.title: Gran Turismo 7
example_games.note: Uses braking points and assist settings as major driving skill factors.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto IV
example_games.note: Uses braking and handbrake behavior during street driving and vehicle control.

example_games.title: Rocket League
example_games.note: Uses braking and reverse input for positioning, recovery, and ball control.

example_games.title: Mario Kart 8
example_games.note: Uses braking and reverse controls in accessible racing scenarios.
